section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports no numbness or tingling.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Absence of numbness/tingling is a patient-reported symptom review rather than an objective exam, so it fits better in the history, but its placement in Findings is a common and not seriously misleading convention.

section_miscategorizations.text: A recent MRI of the brain is pending approval from insurance.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is about test ordering/authorization status rather than a completed result or objective finding, so it aligns more with the management plan, but its current placement is unlikely to substantially mislead.

section_miscategorizations.text: The physician notes that the front office team will work on obtaining pre-authorization for the MRI.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a clear action item regarding coordination of care and test ordering, which belongs in the Plan; leaving it in Findings could obscure that this is an intended next step rather than an observed result.
